mnemonics.title: Articulation Point Rule
mnemonics.content: Root needs 2+ kids, Non-root needs 'low >= disc' for some child. R2K-NR-LGDC: Root 2 Kids - Non-Root Low Greater than Disc

mnemonics.title: Low Value Calculation
mnemonics.content: LOW = min(Discovery, Back edges to ancestors, Children's LOWs). L-D-B-C: Low Depends Back Children

mnemonics.title: Bridge Detection
mnemonics.content: Bridge exists when low[child] > disc[parent]. LBGTD: Low Bigger Greater Than Disc

mnemonics.title: Biconnected Component Extraction
mnemonics.content: Push edges on stack, pop when low >= disc. PPEP: Push Pop Extract Property

acronyms.usage: Timestamp when vertex is first visited in DFS
Minimum disc time reachable from vertex using tree edges and at most one back edge
Traversal method used by Tarjan's algorithm for biconnectivity
Vertex whose removal disconnects the graph

visualTips.visualization: Think of a graph as a network - articulation points are 'hub' cities whose closure cuts airline routes. In a figure-8 shape (two circles touching at one point), the touching vertex is the articulation point.
Picture a tree growing down from root. LOW[u] is how high (to ancestor) you can 'climb up' from u's subtree using at most one back edge. The lower you can go, the more connected you are to ancestors.
Imagine solid blocks (biconnected) connected at hinges (articulation points). You can walk anywhere inside a block without leaving it. Articulation points are doorways between blocks.
A bridge is the only road between two regions - like a drawbridge that cuts off all traffic if raised. Remove it and regions become isolated.

analogies.analogy: Like a single airport that if closed, disconnects flights between some regions. Other airports might still connect within regions, but you can't fly between regions.
Like rooms in a house: you can move between any two points in a room without leaving it (biconnected). Doors (articulation points) connect rooms (components).
Think of LOW[u] as the 'emergency exit' altitude - how high (to root's time) you can escape from u's subtree. If you can escape to an early time, you're well-connected to ancestors.
A bridge is a single-lane road (edge) whose closure disconnects. An articulation point is a toll booth (vertex) whose closure disconnects. Both represent single points of failure.
